headers.//: index.html must be revalidated on every load. It is the file that names the content-hashed bundle, so a cached copy pins the client to an OLD build forever — a new deploy changes the bundle's hash but the stale index.html never learns about it. This is exactly how a shipped fix fails to reach players. Telegram's webview caches heuristically when no directive is present, which is what happened.
Hashed assets are content-addressed: a change produces a new filename, so these can be cached hard and forever.
